subject: Armed-behavior asks closed by filing the disarming lever
action: standing correction: when the deliverable IS the live effect (MONETA receive address / MINT_CHECKOUT_URL, LIMEN_DISPATCH, LIMEN_CENSOR_ISSUES_APPLY), closeout requires the valve ARMED or an irreducible-mint lever naming the real-world action — a beat-wired organ running in permanent dry-run, or an unset env arm, is an UNMET outcome, not a filed one. Enforcement is executable, not prose: RETRO-0708 armed-valve-audit beat check flags any beat-wired organ whose --apply/env arm is never passed; the two-week retro (docs/reviews/retro-2026-06-24--2026-07-08.md, finding 8) is the evidence lineage
authorised_by: operator ask 2026-07-08 — two-week retro: deliberate, critique, heal, evolve toward ideal forms; north stars: make-us-inevitable + pain-point-is-a-product
review: empirical close = armed-valve audit reports zero silently-off valves for a full week: censor mirror actually opening/closing issues, MONETA reachable with a non-empty checkout path or an explicit irreducible lever, LIMEN_DISPATCH decision recorded

subject: Asking the operator a question already decided in a registry
action: standing correction: before surfacing ANY choice to the operator, consult the owning registry (his-hand-levers.json / censor precedents / organ-ladder / pillars). If the decision is homed, DERIVE it — do not ask (asking-in-chat is the same anti-pattern as guessing; [[decisions-owned-by-organ-not-chat]], [[never-need-him-to-speak-again]]). Two corollaries from the originating session: (a) a set already decided elsewhere ('open all lanes') is opened in FULL, never re-picked from a menu; (b) a human-gated boundary is a CONFIGURABLE TIERED VALVE — all tiers coexist, each independently armable, safe defaults — never a one-of-N mode choice. Enforcement is executable, not prose: ask-gate.py DERIVE verdict (PR #758) short-circuits an ask that names an owned lever/precedent; the tiered-valve ideal-form is spec/outbound-valve.json audited by armed-valve-audit.
authorised_by: operator 2026-07-08 — 'we've decided this before and elsewhere—like all things' + 'why is it one or the other? all of the above always (configurable)'
review: empirical close = ask-gate DERIVE fires on registry-owned asks in the beat log AND no operator-facing menu recurs for an already-decided question for a full week

action: resolved by existing organ — no new MCP server: GitHub access is the gh CLI organ (keyring-derive self-heal #251), scripts/mcp-auth-verify.py already probes MCP auth each beat, and the ianva doorway levers L-IANVA-LOCAL/#262 + L-IANVA-CLOUD/#263 own the MCP surface; a GitHub MCP server would duplicate a healthy organ

subject: Full local gate matrix run on docs-class branches
action: standing correction encoded: CLAUDE.md § Worktree Isolation & CI Gate Matrix scoping rule — scripts/verify-scoped.sh is the default push gate (maps changed paths to only the implicated gates and names every skip); the full verify-whole.sh is a pre-merge event for deploy-trigger/cross-component diffs, machine-serialized via LIMEN_VERIFY_LOCK_FILE; CI (pr-gate.yml + path-filtered ci.yml) remains the full-matrix layer and merge-policy.sh enforces the website guardrail; zero local gates is never an option
authorised_by: operator correction 2026-07-09 — killed a full local verify-whole on a four-file docs/data diff; full-matrix-on-every-branch is duplicated work, not extra safety
review: empirical close = docs-class branches ship with owning-predicate-only local runs and green PR CI; re-open if a scoped-gate branch ever merges a regression CI would not have caught

subject: Verification-gate estate duplicated across 8+ surfaces
action: gates-as-data ideal form landed across four PRs (#780 registry + check-gates.py, #787 verify.py resolver, #792 merge-policy derives, PR4 verify-whole derives + charter pointer): institutio/governance/gates.yaml is the single declaration of every gate (command, implicating paths, tier, serialize, scoped, ci_job); scripts/verify.py selects and executes (scoped and file-set expansion); merge-policy.sh and verify-whole.sh derive instead of hardcoding; check-gates.py holds registry<->workflows<->consumers parity on every PR via pr-gate; the merge-policy awk staleness guard and the CLAUDE.md keep-in-lockstep chore are deleted along with the duplication they guarded; consumer ratchets prevent literal copies from returning
authorised_by: operator question 2026-07-09 'what is the logical ideal form of all of this?' following the crash-the-computer full-matrix correction (PREC-2026-07-09-scoped-gates); hand lists drifted three times during the build itself
review: empirical close = a quarter with zero manual lockstep edits: every gate addition lands as one registry entry and drift surfaces as a red check-gates run, never as session memory

subject: Monitoring reported healthy while the fleet idled all night
action: throughput becomes a gated predicate, not an afterthought: emit-tick.py stamps done_delta (completed = done + archived) on every beat (#802); overnight-watch.py gains a throughput-collapse alert whose floor is DERIVED from the trailing 7-day per-window median (LIMEN_THROUGHPUT_FLOOR_FRACTION x median), never a pinned count, and which fires only on genuine silent stall — open work present AND no sanctioned suppression (governor pause, vitals shed, budget exhaustion, dispatch gate), each surfaced as throughput.suppressed rather than hidden; graduated vitals backpressure (#786) stops the warn-level full-skip that caused 273 idle beats; the async cap is derived from host capacity (#779) so it is never hand-throttled to 1 again
authorised_by: operator fury 2026-07-09 'what are we fucking doing... all you're tracking is movement, as opposed to actual things being positive and completing' — a green heartbeat with 12 merges vs a 23-40/day baseline is an idling engine reported as healthy
review: empirical close = a throughput-collapse fires (or is correctly suppressed) on the next real stall, and no night passes with liveness green while completion is near-zero

subject: Alerts whose only consumer is the operator
action: every alert this monitor owns names its effector (overnight-watch.py heal()): heartbeat-launchd-not-running -> bootstrap; plist-drift -> reinstall the committed plist + reload (the Jul-7 hand-edit-starves-the-fleet class); throughput-collapse -> kickstart, and a collapse that survives a prior remediation escalates to the censor GitHub issues mirror with dedup — NEVER to the operator in chat; the same rule retired the approval-ask reflex (deciding a cap, an acceptance, or a schedule in chat is routing a derivable decision to him). Memory: sensor-without-effector-defect.md
authorised_by: operator 2026-07-09 'why do i have to KEEP FUCKING BRINGING THIS TO THE TABLE?' rejecting both a plan-approval ask and cap/acceptance questions — encode the ideal form, do not route derivable decisions or unremediated alerts back to the human
review: empirical close = a quarter in which every monitor alert resolves via a receipt-backed effector or an auto-filed issue, and no fleet decision or alert lands in chat as an ask

subject: "Nothing ships" was a CI-jam: the autonomic direct-push lane committed SOURCE to main with no PR, so pr-gate (full tests+mypy+ruff) sat chronically red and blocked every human PR
action: The sanctioned direct-push lane is DATA-only by charter but nothing enforced it: capture.sh:163 `git add -A` (excludes only secrets+tasks.yaml) and continuation-beat.py:123 bare `git push` of a caller path-list both let .py reach main un-CI'd (the capacity.py regression, the opencode f-string SyntaxError, ruff-format drift, undeclared LIMEN_ESTATE_* params). Effector = make the declared scope executable: (1) a DATA_ONLY guard at the 3 chokepoints (capture.sh unstage-source, continuation-beat.commit_paths pre-push assert, tabularius assert) that refuses+surfaces source, never silently commits it; (2) wire the already-existing omega.sh:93 main-green sensor -> censor residual -> GitHub issue (via sync-censor-issues.py) + a self-heal task for main (self-heal.py today heals PRs only, never the trunk). Owed engineering: limen#872. Memory pointer: direct-push-lane-rots-main-ci.md.
authorised_by: operator 2026-07-10 'find what exists, evolve it--make it so i dont have to fucking ask again' and then 'How do we make sure none of this happens again? Who owns this information?' — the ideal form is executable ownership: the lane's data-only scope enforced by a guard, a red trunk self-surfacing as an issue, never discovered by a human days later
review: empirical close = a quarter in which no source file reaches main except through a merged PR (the DATA_ONLY guard has refused >=1 real source-leak, logged), and main never sits pr-gate-red for >2 beats without an auto-filed censor issue + a self-heal task — no CI-jam recurs

close_conditions.description: Machine-checkable: the DATA_ONLY guard tests pass (capture.sh + continuation-beat + tabularius chokepoints all verified). Empirical: no source file reaches main outside a PR for a full quarter; main never sits pr-gate-red for >2 beats without an auto-filed issue + self-heal task.

subject: Two sessions repaired the SAME red trunk in parallel (this session's green-main agent vs another session's PR #871) — wasted work because claim-dedup is by task-ID, not by work-intent
action: dispatch.py release_stale_tasks() dedups by session_id + stale age, but nothing prevents two agents fixing 'main is red' under different task IDs. Effector = route trunk-red repair through ONE claimable keyed HEAL task (self-heal.py already emits keyed HEAL-cifix/HEAL-rebase tasks; extend the key space to a HEAL-<repo>-<symptom> singleton for the trunk) so the second session sees it claimed and skips. Ties to the A1 wiring in PREC-2026-07-10-direct-push-lane-rots-main.
authorised_by: operator 2026-07-10 'Do we need a repository to own this or an organ to own this? ... How do we make sure none of this happens again?' — the dedup gap is a real hanging owner: nobody owns 'this repair is already being worked'
review: empirical close = a quarter with no two PRs/agents fixing the same trunk-red symptom concurrently; the keyed HEAL claim is observed absorbing duplicate repair attempts in the beat log

close_conditions.description: Machine-checkable: the HEAL-<repo>-<symptom> singleton dedup tests pass (two invocations for the same repo+symptom yield ONE task id). Empirical: no two sessions produce parallel repair PRs for the same trunk-red symptom for a full quarter.

subject: The mechanical task ('push the 8 owed mail drafts to Gmail') would have COMPLETED while making things worse — the drafts were hollow auto-stubs and 5/8 were newsletters/receipts/recruiter-blasts wrongly flagged reply-owed
action: Refused to dump 8 empty '[Add your specifics here]' stubs into his Drafts (that is the toiling-on-nothing he was furious about). Engaged the real problem: the UMA classifier manufactures false reply-owed obligations from bulk mail. Fixed at source (organvm/universal-mail--automation#161, MERGED): bulk-signal headers (List-Unsubscribe/List-Id/Precedence) suppress the personal reply-owed rung end-to-end; genuinely reply-worthy mail (litigation/interview/technical) stays flagged. Reinforces CLAUDE.md 'Engage the Real Problem First' — a task that 'completes' while degrading the outcome is not done.
authorised_by: operator 2026-07-10 'Why is none of my mail getting completed?' — completing his mail meant fixing the noise factory, not producing more noise; the charter's shallow-first friction (PREC-2026-07-04-friction-shallow-first) in a new guise
review: empirical close = a quarter in which no bulk/newsletter sender appears as a reply-owed obligation, and no hollow-template draft is ever written to a real mailbox

subject: The whole storage-deadlock session was ONE meta-defect — declared-but-unwired / silently-red: LIMEN_RECLAIM_PUSHED_OK was declared in parameters.yaml with a note promising a reap but read by no source; reap-clones.py existed but was never beat-wired; and trunk pr-gate was red for an unknown period while the entire PR queue silently could not merge, with no alarm that trunk itself was broken.
action: Turned both blind spots into EXECUTABLE predicates (Definition-of-Done law, not prose), extending existing organs in limen — no new repo. (1) Wiring integrity: check-params.py gained the inverse direction — a declared LIMEN_* param read by NO source fails pr-gate, ratcheted like the undeclared baseline (PR #881; retro-proven to catch a LIMEN_RECLAIM_PUSHED_OK-shaped orphan). (2) Trunk-CI health: scripts/trunk-ci-health.py, a HEAL-beat sensor that alarms when >=K FRESH open PRs share one failing REQUIRED check — the trunk-red fingerprint omega's main-green rung structurally misses (it probes gh run list --branch main, but pr-gate fires on pull_request, never on push), with a self-owned auto-closing issue mirror, dark by default (PR #882). Two reflexes: 'a declared param/organ with no reader or caller is a DEFECT, not a stub' and 'when a green PR will not merge, check whether TRUNK's required pr-gate is red FIRST — the fix is healing the base, not rebasing each PR.'
authorised_by: operator 2026-07-10 'How do we make sure none of this happens again? Should this be owned by any other repository? Is the repository built that should own it, or should we build it?' — answered: limen (the SSOT + VIGILIA autonomic institution) owns it; the guard/gate/effector machinery already exists, so the fix is extending two organs, not a new repo.
review: empirical close = a quarter in which check-params' orphan baseline never regresses (no new declared-but-unread param merges) AND trunk-ci-health is observed opening then auto-closing its alarm around a real trunk-red episode in the beat log.

subject: Closed PR #882 as a 'duplicate' of check-main-green.py instead of landing its value — the operator: 'this is unacceptable — PRs don't get closed — they get solved.'
action: Re-examined #882 on the correction: its queue-wedge signal was ADDITIVE, not redundant (live: 'WEDGE (trunk green) — 11 fresh PRs blocked on pr-gate', a divergence check-main-green's ci.yml-on-main read structurally cannot see). Integrated it into the OWNING organ and merged (#893 Closes #882) — blast-radius on the RED alarm + a green-but-wedged predicate; standalone duplicate file not reintroduced. Standing law: a PR is SOLVED (merge / integrate-value-into-owner-and-merge / supersede-with-Closes-#N), NEVER closed-as-duplicate-and-abandoned. If GitHub won't reopen a reworked branch, open a superseding PR that Closes #N. Only the operator vetoes a close without a merge. Same reflex as the compliant gate-reroute: reduce to the landable atom, never dead-stop.
authorised_by: operator 2026-07-10 'this is unacceptable--PRs dont get closed--they get solved'
review: empirical close = a quarter with zero PRs closed as duplicate/obsolete without their value landed via a merge (integrate/supersede), auditable from the PR graph

subject: Nearly wired a new effector (trunk-red escalation, limen#872 A1) to WRITE logs/censor-residual.json — but that file is REGENERATED every beat by censor.py from the insights lineage, so the write would have been silently clobbered next beat: a change that LOOKS wired but is a no-op
action: Caught before shipping by verifying the file's contract first (git grep writers: censor.py owns+regenerates it; sync-censor-issues.py only reads). Standing rule: before writing to any shared state file, confirm whether it is a DERIVED/regenerated projection or a genuine append/event target — a derived file has exactly one writer (its generator) and must be fed at the SOURCE, never appended to at the sink. The correct home for a trunk-red escalation is overnight-watch.py's existing direct escalate_issue() (create/close a GH issue by marker, no derived-file involved) OR censor.py's own regeneration input — filed on the precedent, not shipped as the clobbered version. Sibling of the board-projection rule (tasks.yaml is a projection; write via tabularius, not the sink).
authorised_by: operator 2026-07-10 'make it so i dont have to fucking ask again ... how do we make sure none of this happens again' — shipping a silently-clobbered effector is worse than the gap it closes, because it reads as done; the recurrence guard is 'verify the file's writer-topology before writing', not another organ
review: empirical close = a quarter in which no effector is merged that writes to a generator-owned derived file at its sink (would-be-clobbered); the derived-vs-append distinction is checked at design time, observed in review comments citing this precedent

action: created censor/insights-suggestions-raw-2026-07-17.json serializing complete per-report suggestion extraction for all 6 reports; updated insights-suggestions.jsonl to 36 rows covering all stamps; appended precedent row; sibling builder ships cross-lane block to AGENTS.md
